content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM and RND from Topic A) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data gets stored into an active learning agent’s long-term memory, creating a self-organizing memory system that automatically retains high-value, information-dense samples for future learning rather than storing all encountered data. This resolves the current SOTA challenge of scaling active learning agent memory by aligning memory retention with the agent’s own intrinsic learning progress.
